prose: Fukui reactivity analysis on acrolein (8 atoms: O, C, C, C, H, H, H, H) computed at DFT tier 'standard' → B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF RKS, Mulliken charge scheme), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied acrolein.xyz, used as-is (single point, not re-optimized). Cation (charge +1, mult 2) and anion (charge −1, mult 2) single-point calculations were used to form finite-difference Fukui indices. SCF converged; integrity status OK, trustworthy: true. Charge conservation: sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000 (both pass). No warnings reported. Headline result: most electrophilic atom is C4 (β-carbon) with f+ = 0.64232102; most nucleophilic atom is O1 (carbonyl oxygen) with f− = 0.39388616. The large positive dual descriptor on C4 (+0.5550) confirms it as the dominant Michael acceptor site, consistent with acrolein's conjugated carbonyl chemistry.
